intro: Investors including Chamath Palihapitiya are warning that excessive AI token spending will pressure corporate earnings as the era of "tokenmaxxing" winds down. New York became the first U.S. state to ban new large-scale AI data center construction after Governor Kathy Hochul signed an executive order. Spotify launched a ChatGPT-like conversational assistant for Premium subscribers to discover music and podcasts, while Apple released the iOS 27 public beta featuring its redesigned AI-powered Siri.

whitelist.title: Chamath Palihapitiya says soaring AI token spend will hit companies' earnings
whitelist.desc: Palihapitiya is part of a growing chorus of investors and tech executives who are cautioning that the tokenmaxxing era is coming to an end.

whitelist.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to impose AI data center ban
whitelist.desc: New York became the first U.S. state to ban the construction of new large-scale AI data centers after Governor Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday.

whitelist.title: Three publishers challenge Google over AI copyright infringement - Engadget
whitelist.desc: It’s the latest in a barrage of efforts to win compensation from AI companies over training materials.

whitelist.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, and audiobooks, and more.

whitelist.title: Global cooperation needed to tackle AI threats, says Bank of England governor
whitelist.desc: Andrew Bailey warns that US will not be able to achieve its ambitions alone

whitelist.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

whitelist.title: Eye on AI
whitelist.desc: Fortune's Eye On AI newsletter explores how AI is rapidly transforming business—chronicling the companies pioneering artificial intelligence technology, from OpenAI and Anthropic to Google and Microsoft, how the Fortune 500 is addressing AI's opportunities and challenges, and AI's impact on the wider economy and society. Sign up here.

whitelist.title: Anthropic Launches AI For Teachers
whitelist.desc: Anthropic launches Claude for Teachers, offering free AI tools aligned to standards, curriculum, privacy requirements and everyday classroom workloads nationwide.

whitelist.title: The UK wants to catch up in the global AI race – but is too wary of risks to go all-in
whitelist.desc: UK fears a ‘triple whammy’: oversized investment in AI stocks, slower adoption of AI than predicted and the breakneck pace of AI’s development

whitelist.title: D-topia review – cosy sci-fi mystery takes aim at AI
whitelist.desc: A soft puzzle game makes a sharp point about the over-optimised future ahead

whitelist.title: Google brings Gemini in Chrome to UK users - Engadget
whitelist.desc: Chrome’s Gemini integration has arrived in the UK.

whitelist.title: Gemini in Chrome is rolling out in the UK.
whitelist.desc: It’s taken over a year, but starting today, British Chrome users can access Google’s chatbot interface directly in the Chrome browser to compare information across multiple tabs, summarize content, and ask questions about what’s on their open web pages. It still isn’t available in the EU, however. [Image: Here’s what the Gemini interface looks like when comparing Jet2Holiday listings on Chrome. Cos that’s what Brits do, innit. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Scre...

whitelist.title: OpenAI's new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

whitelist.title: Meta’s AI can now dub your voice in more languages on Instagram and Facebook.
whitelist.desc: The AI translations are expanding to Japanese, Korean, French, German, and Italian on Instagram and Bahasa, Indonesian, Arabic, French, Vietnamese, and Thai on Facebook, according to Instagram boss Adam Mosseri. [Media: https://www.instagram.com/p/DaxpKQfRaSV/]

whitelist.title: Boko Haram exploited US and Chinese AI chatbots for attacks: Cambridge study
whitelist.desc: Former fighters say AI helped with bomb-making, planning and propaganda, raising calls for US-China action on terrorist misuse of chatbots.

whitelist.title: Morning Mail: what worries young people most, PM unveils AI plan, Spain into World Cup final
whitelist.desc: Study reveals gen Z’s priorities along with distrust of politicians and media;

whitelist.title: Former employees sue over Meta's alleged use of biased AI systems during layoffs - Engadget
whitelist.desc: Meta cut its workforce by 10 percent in May.

whitelist.title: OpenAI’s first hardware product sounds more like a companion than a speaker
whitelist.desc: OpenAI is reportedly developing a screen-free AI smart speaker that acts as a human-like companion, combining ChatGPT, advanced sensors, and a proactive AI assistant in its first consumer hardware product.

whitelist.title: OpenAI has a new statement about Apple’s lawsuit.
whitelist.desc: Here it is, given to Bloomberg: > While we take these allegations seriously, we’re not aware of any evidence that this complaint has merit. We believe in fair competition and allowing people the freedom to work wherever they choose, and we’re focused on building innovative technology that empowers people everywhere. Apple sued OpenAI on Friday, alleging the company stole hardware secrets.

whitelist.title: OpenAI and Anthropic are fighting over college students with free AI
whitelist.desc: OpenAI and Anthropic launch dual education initiatives to court college students.

whitelist.title: Can AI audiobooks narrate better than humans? This study says many listeners think so
whitelist.desc: A surprising new study shows people rate AI narrated audiobooks as more engaging and higher quality than human ones.

whitelist.title: Employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to make layoffs
whitelist.desc: The lawsuit filed by current and former Meta employees underscores rising concerns about AI's impact on jobs and people with disabilities in the workforce.

whitelist.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
whitelist.desc: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

whitelist.title: What smart people are saying about IBM's AI warning and SaaSpocalypse fears
whitelist.desc: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna wrote a letter to shareholders about a revenue miss. It's renewing months-old fears about a SaaSpocalypse.

whitelist.title: Meta used AI workplace tools to target employees on medical leave, lawsuit alleges
whitelist.desc: Meta faces a lawsuit from 26 employees alleging AI systems unfairly targeted them for layoffs for taking medical leave.

whitelist.title: Anthropic's newest ad is creeping people out | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.

whitelist.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."

whitelist.title: Logistics company C.H. Robinson achieved a 45% productivity gain with AI agents. Here's the secrets of its success. | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Robinson CEO Dave Bozeman says its one part tech, and equal parts organization and culture.

whitelist.title: The AI boom drove China's 27% export jump in June as AI and the Iran war reshape global trade | Fortune
whitelist.desc: China's exports of vehicles, especially EVs, and other tech-related products have boomed as rapid adoption of AI increases the need for semiconductors and other electronic equipment.

whitelist.title: 'Those who condemn AI are themselves spitting upward': SoftBank's CEO estimates $5 trillion needed annually to meet AI demand | Fortune
whitelist.desc: Masayoshi Son founded SoftBank more than four decades ago and was an early supporter of AI, investing tens of billions of dollars to related companies.

whitelist.title: Today’s Vergecast: The problem with Suno and AI music.
whitelist.desc: AI-generated music is suddenly everywhere. On social media, music streaming services, and elsewhere, stuff made on Suno is becoming unavoidable. The Verge’s Terrence O’Brien joins David to talk about where Suno came from, whether it can truly change the way we create and consume music, and how we can and should respond to AI music. We also have some thoughts on whether these songs might someday be bangers. Watch | Listen | Get ad-free [Media: https://youtu.be/UyXYAbHpbTs]

whitelist.title: Caution warranted on this red hot AI cloud data stock that's now 'priced for perfection,' says Todd Gordon
whitelist.desc: Datadog runs the control room for modern software: it pulls data from a company's servers, apps and cloud services into one place.

whitelist.title: Spotify’s new conversational AI can play tracks you request and answer your music questions
whitelist.desc: Spotify is rolling out a conversational AI feature that can play requested tracks, answer music questions, and refine selections through follow-up prompts.

whitelist.title: Cybersecurity stocks rally on AI spending change comments from IBM's Krishna
whitelist.desc: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna told CNBC's Sara Eisen that some major deals were put on hold toward the end of the quarter as businesses rethink spending.

whitelist.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.

whitelist.title: Gemini can make sense of the world around you, but don’t let it observe your children just yet
whitelist.desc: Researchers tested Gemini 2.5 Pro on parent-child interaction videos and found AI is reliable at observation but falls short when making expert-level judgments about child communication.

whitelist.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI | TechCrunch
whitelist.desc: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

whitelist.title: Anthropic introduced a Claude product for K-12 teachers.
whitelist.desc: “Claude for Teachers” gives verified US educators complimentary Claude access, including “a library of teaching skills and a direct connection to evidence-based curricula, mapped to academic standards in all 50 states,” Anthropic wrote. It’s part of a recent push by both Anthropic and OpenAI to introduce education-specific AI tools, for university students and beyond. [Link: Claude (@claudeai) on X | https://x.com/claudeai/status/2077047278078931243?s=20 | X (formerly Twitter)]

whitelist.title: IBM shares plunge after preliminary Q2 revenue falls short of estimates despite surging AI bookings
whitelist.desc: IBM issued preliminary Q2 results showing revenue of $17 billion, below analyst estimates, as clients shifted spending and large deals failed to close.

whitelist.title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
whitelist.desc: A group of former Meta employees is suing the company over claims that it used AI tools to choose which workers to lay off, including those on medical leave.

whitelist.title: ChatGPT is now citing Kalshi data.
whitelist.desc: OpenAI and the prediction market have a deal, The New York Times reports, that pulls in Kalshi data for World Cup-related queries. A prompt for “France and Spain,” for example, included a win forecast attributed to Kalshi. Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket are everywhere right now, and that’s by design. The companies have inked deals with news outlets, newsletter writers, and random X accounts — and now Kalshi will reach potential customers via chatbots. [Image: https://platform.thev...
